_comment: Common-form anti-pattern rules for the corpus linter. Each rule encodes a (regex, label) constraint: tokens matching the regex either MUST be one of `allowed_labels` (anything else is flagged), or MUST NOT be any of `forbidden_labels`. Add patterns here as new failure modes surface. Severity: 'error' blocks unless acknowledged; 'warn' surfaces in report but doesn't gate.

rules.message: Digit+ordinal token (5th, 12th) labeled as a non-house-number non-street tag. This is the 5th Avenue Theatre failure mode — synth-no-street v0.6.2 included 'venue-adversarial' templates starting with these tokens, which confused the model about house_number recognition. Legitimate uses: B-house_number, I-street (as part of '5th Avenue'), B-street, O.

Two-char all-caps token (CA, NY, MA, OR, ND) labeled as a non-admin tag. These are almost always US region abbreviations or alphanumeric postcode prefixes. Misclassification is a signal of confused training data.
Five-digit token (potential US ZIP) labeled as non-postcode non-house_number. Allowed: B-postcode, I-postcode, B-house_number (for cases like '12345 Main St'), O.
